companies.name: Cursor
companies.description: AI code editor. $29.3B valuation, $1B+ ARR, 2M+ users. Fastest-growing SaaS product in history by some measures. 1,000% YoY growth per Brex data.

companies.name: Lovable
companies.description: AI app builder (Swedish). $200M raise in July 2025. One of the fastest revenue ramps in software history. 'Vibe coding' wave leader.

companies.name: Bolt
companies.description: AI full-stack app generation in-browser. Direct Lovable competitor, huge dev adoption.

companies.name: Windsurf
companies.description: Wild 2025 saga: OpenAI tried to buy for $3B, deal collapsed, Google poached founders for $2.4B, Cognition acquired remaining IP for $250M. Still ranked #1 on LogRocket AI Dev Tool Rankings.

companies.name: Cognition
companies.description: Autonomous AI software engineer. $400M raise at $10.2B valuation (Sep 2025). Grew from $1M to $73M ARR in 9 months.

companies.name: Vercel
companies.description: Next.js platform, default frontend deploy layer. $250M+ raised, ubiquitous in modern web dev.

companies.name: Wiz
companies.description: Cloud security, bottoms-up with security teams. Acquired by Google for ~$32B. One of the biggest SaaS exits ever, in just ~4 years.

companies.name: Supabase
companies.description: Open-source Firebase alternative. Series C, $500M+ valuation. Beloved developer brand.

companies.name: Neon
companies.description: Serverless Postgres. Strong PLG motion, developer darling.

companies.name: Replit
companies.description: AI-powered coding/app platform. $250M Series C in 2025. Big in education + prototyping.

companies.name: Linear
companies.description: Project management that spread virally through eng teams. Cult-favorite brand.

companies.name: Resend
companies.description: Email API for devs. Beautiful DX, very fast early traction.

companies.name: Val Town
companies.description: Lightweight cloud scripting, cult following.

companies.name: Tonic.ai
companies.description: Synthetic data for dev/test environments. $47M raised, 500% YoY growth.

companies.name: Stainless
companies.description: Platform for high-quality APIs. Sequoia + a16z Series A. Used by OpenAI, Anthropic, others for their SDKs.

companies.name: Render
companies.description: Cloud platform alternative to AWS/Heroku for indie devs and startups. Strong bottoms-up.

companies.name: Turso
companies.description: Edge database (libSQL). Developer-first, growing fast in the local-first/edge movement.

companies.name: Depot
companies.description: Fast container builds for CI/CD. Small but fast-growing dev infra.

companies.name: Fly.io
companies.description: Edge app hosting. Strong developer community, very PLG.

companies.name: Gamma
companies.description: AI-powered presentations/docs. Exploded virally, millions of users. Not pure dev tool but extremely PLG.

companies.name: Granola
companies.description: AI meeting notes. Classic PLG viral loop through team sharing.

companies.name: Raycast
companies.description: Productivity launcher (Mac). Replaced Alfred/Spotlight for devs, growing into a platform.

companies.name: Databricks
companies.description: $134B valuation, $4.8B revenue run rate, 55%+ YoY growth. The enterprise data/AI platform. Raised $4B Series L.

companies.name: Stripe
companies.description: Still private, still growing, still the payments default. IPO speculation constant.

companies.name: Vanta
companies.description: Automated compliance. $2.45B valuation. Every startup uses it.

companies.name: Rippling
companies.description: HR/IT/finance compound startup. $13B+ valuation. Parker Conrad's compound startup thesis.

companies.name: Ramp
companies.description: Corporate cards and spend management. Growing faster than Brex.

companies.name: Glean
companies.description: Enterprise AI search. $4.6B valuation. Selling to Fortune 500.

companies.name: Harvey
companies.description: AI for legal. $300M Series D at $3B valuation (Feb 2025). Sequoia-led. Used by top law firms globally.

companies.name: Mercor
companies.description: AI hiring/talent matching. Exploded to $2B+ valuation at insane speed.

companies.name: Sierra
companies.description: Bret Taylor + Clay Bavor. AI customer service. $4B+ valuation pre-PMF on founder pedigree alone.

companies.name: Hebbia
companies.description: AI for finance/knowledge work. $130M Series B from a16z.

companies.name: Writer
companies.description: Enterprise generative AI platform. 'Safe' enterprise LLM layer positioning.

companies.name: Decagon
companies.description: AI customer support automation. $250M raise (March 2026), half a billion total. Coatue + Index co-led.

companies.name: Clay
companies.description: GTM data enrichment/automation. Viral among revenue teams. Classic PLG-to-enterprise motion.

companies.name: Celonis
companies.description: Process mining at scale. 700+ enterprise customers. European breakout.

companies.name: Gong
companies.description: Revenue intelligence. Still dominant in the conversation intelligence space.

companies.name: Fivetran
companies.description: Data integration/ETL. Quiet giant, massive enterprise footprint.

companies.name: Gusto
companies.description: HR/payroll for SMBs. Steady compounder with massive market.

companies.name: Uniphore
companies.description: Enterprise AI. $260M Series F at $2.5B valuation (Oct 2025). Nvidia, Snowflake, Databricks as investors.

companies.name: Legora
companies.description: Legal AI (Swedish). Hit unicorn status at $1.8B after raising $150M. 400+ law firms across 40 markets.

companies.name: 7AI
companies.description: AI cybersecurity agents. $130M Series A (Dec 2025). Index Ventures led. Founded 2024, already massive round.

companies.name: Polymarket
companies.description: Prediction markets. Huge 2024 election moment, now a real platform.

companies.name: Kalshi
companies.description: Prediction/event contracts exchange.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.description: $500B valuation (Oct 2025 secondary). $40B raise in March 2025. Projecting $20B ARR by 2026.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.description: $183B valuation. $14.3B+ total funding including $13B Series F (Sep 2025). Claude. Safety-focused frontier lab.

companies.name: xAI
companies.description: Elon's lab. $20B raise in Jan 2026 from Nvidia, Cisco, Fidelity. ~$200B estimated valuation.

companies.name: Mistral
companies.description: French AI lab. $6B+ valuation. Open-weight models challenging US dominance. Nvidia + ASML backed.

companies.name: Perplexity
companies.description: AI-native search. $9B+ valuation. Genuine threat to Google's core product.

companies.name: Cohere
companies.description: Enterprise LLM provider. Strong in RAG and enterprise deployments.

companies.name: ElevenLabs
companies.description: AI voice generation. $180M Series C at $3B+ valuation (Jan 2025). Went viral, scaled incredibly fast.

companies.name: Runway
companies.description: AI video generation. Used in Hollywood. Pioneer of the category.

companies.name: Poolside
companies.description: $500M at $3B valuation before launching a product.

companies.name: Sesame
companies.description: Voice AI. $250M Series B co-led by Sequoia and Spark (Oct 2025). Went viral with realistic voice demos.

companies.name: Fal
companies.description: Generative media platform. $140M Series D at $4.5B (Dec 2025). Sequoia-led.

companies.name: Unconventional AI
companies.description: Rethinking computing foundations for AI. $475M seed round (Dec 2025) at ~$4.5B. Lightspeed + a16z.

companies.name: Suno
companies.description: AI music creation. Raised fundamental questions about authorship. Fast consumer adoption.

companies.name: LMArena
companies.description: AI evaluation/benchmarking platform. $1.7B valuation in under 4 months. $150M Series A (Jan 2026).

companies.name: Genspark
companies.description: All-in-one AI workspace. $275M Series B at $1.25B (Nov 2025).

companies.name: Fireworks AI
companies.description: Open-source model deployment platform. $250M Series C at $4B (Oct 2025).

companies.name: SSI
companies.description: Ilya Sutskever's post-OpenAI company. $1B raised pre-product. Pure research bet on superintelligence safety.

companies.name: Hippocratic AI
companies.description: LLMs for healthcare. $141M Series B at $1.6B+ (Jan 2025). Kleiner Perkins led.

companies.name: Sakana AI
companies.description: Tokyo-based, nature-inspired AI research. Founded by ex-Google Brain researchers. Raised $300M+.

companies.name: Deepgram
companies.description: Enterprise voice AI. $130M Series C (Jan 2026).

companies.name: Merge Labs
companies.description: Sam Altman's brain-computer interface startup. $250M seed round in early 2026. OpenAI as lead investor.

companies.name: Physical Intelligence
companies.description: Robot foundation models. $400M at $2.4B in first year.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.description: GPU cloud. Pivoted from crypto mining to $35B+ IPO. One of the wildest trajectory stories in tech.

companies.name: Groq
companies.description: Custom LPU inference chips. $2.8B valuation. Went viral with speed demos.

companies.name: Base Power Company
companies.description: Distributed natural gas generators for data centers. $200M+ raised, $2.5B+ valuation in ~2 years. Austin-based.

companies.name: Saronic
companies.description: Autonomous naval vessels. $600M+ raised (a16z Series C), $4B valuation. Austin-based.

companies.name: Cerebras
companies.description: Wafer-scale AI chips. Filed to IPO. Competing with Nvidia on inference.

companies.name: Together AI
companies.description: AI cloud inference/training. $3B+ valuation.

companies.name: Lambda
companies.description: GPU cloud for AI training. $800M raised.

companies.name: Gecko Robotics
companies.description: Wall-climbing infrastructure inspection robots. Unicorn status June 2025 at $1.25B. Founders Fund + YC backed. Pittsburgh.

companies.name: Radiant Nuclear
companies.description: Portable nuclear microreactors for clean energy. El Segundo, CA.

companies.name: Astranis
companies.description: Small, low-cost telecom satellites for remote internet. Raised $350M+.

companies.name: Skild AI
companies.description: Foundation models for robotics. $1.4B growth round in Jan 2026. One of the largest robotics raises ever.

companies.name: Arc Boat Company
companies.description: Electric vessels. $50M Series C (March 2026). Moving from consumer to defense/commercial. a16z backed.

companies.name: Helion Energy
companies.description: Fusion power. Backed by Sam Altman. Building the world's first fusion power plant (contracted with Microsoft).

companies.name: QuEra Computing
companies.description: Quantum computing. $230M Series B in 2025.

companies.name: HawkEye 360
companies.description: RF geospatial intelligence from space. $150M Series E (late 2025). Defense + commercial.

companies.name: Sierra Space
companies.description: Orbital vehicles and space infrastructure. Defense + NASA contracts.

companies.name: Claros
companies.description: Chip-level data center power infrastructure. $30M seed (March 2026). Former IBM engineers.

companies.name: Icarus
companies.description: Autonomous solar-powered aircraft at 60,000 feet. Modern U-2 spy plane, $100K each, built by the thousands. YC-backed.

companies.name: Sorcerer
companies.description: High-altitude balloon weather sensors. 1000x more data than existing systems. Already used daily by meteorologists.

companies.name: Velontra
companies.description: Hypersonic space plane with horizontal takeoff. Air-breathing propulsion 6x more fuel efficient than rockets.

companies.name: SpaceX
companies.description: #1 on Setter 30 Q4 2025. $800B tender valuation. Possible IPO in 2026. Starship + Starlink era.

companies.name: Anduril
companies.description: Palmer Luckey's defense tech company. $30.5B valuation. Lattice OS + autonomous drones/missiles.

companies.name: Figure AI
companies.description: Humanoid robotics. $39B valuation (Sep 2025). Fastest path to unicorn in robotics history. Robots deployed at BMW.

companies.name: 1X Technologies
companies.description: Humanoid home robots (NEO). 10,000-unit deployment deal with EQT (2026-2030). Pre-orders at $20,000.

companies.name: Agility Robotics
companies.description: Digit humanoid robot. World's first full-scale humanoid robot factory (RoboFab). Deployed at Amazon + Spanx.

companies.name: Apptronik
companies.description: Humanoid robotics. Austin-based.

companies.name: Hadrian
companies.description: AI-powered precision manufacturing for defense/aerospace. $200M+ Series C.

companies.name: Impulse Space
companies.description: In-space orbital delivery. Tom Mueller (SpaceX co-founder). $300M+ raised.

companies.name: Relativity Space
companies.description: 3D-printed rockets. Pivoted to Terran R. $1.2B+ raised.

companies.name: Shield AI
companies.description: Autonomous defense drones with Hivemind AI pilot. $200M+ raised, $2.8B valuation.

companies.name: Hermeus
companies.description: Hypersonic aircraft. Mach 5+ autonomous planes for defense and commercial. USAF contracts.

companies.name: Saildrone
companies.description: Autonomous ocean vehicles for defense, climate, and maritime data. 100+ vehicles deployed globally.

companies.name: Vast
companies.description: Commercial space station (Haven-1). Launching the first commercial space station.

companies.name: Joby Aviation
companies.description: eVTOL air taxi. Publicly traded. FAA certification path, Toyota-backed manufacturing.

companies.name: Archer Aviation
companies.description: eVTOL competitor to Joby. Manufacturing facility in Georgia. United Airlines partnership.

companies.name: Reliable Robotics
companies.description: Autonomous cargo aircraft. FAA certification pursuit for pilotless commercial flights.

companies.name: Redwood Materials
companies.description: Battery recycling. JB Straubel (Tesla co-founder). $1B+ raised.

companies.name: Zetta Energy
companies.description: Next-gen AI chips ('XPUs'). Building the next Nvidia with reconfigurable, energy-efficient processors. YC-backed.

companies.name: Northvolt
companies.description: European battery manufacturing giant. Raised 14 rounds. Building Europe's battery supply chain independence.

companies.name: Oklo
companies.description: Advanced fission nuclear. Sam Altman as chairman. Public via SPAC. Micro-reactor approach for data centers.
